evidence_excerpt: At last follow-up, she can walk independently using a 7 cm shoelift; the right hip is well-reduced, but there is recurrence of equinovarus deformity needing additional surgical correction and persistent leg length discrepancy/hypotrophy.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence: Independent ambulation with a 7 cm shoelift; leg length discrepancy (partly due to pelvic obliquity), hypotrophy of the right lower limb, and a stiff foot indicate ongoing functional limitation.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence: Recurrence of equinovarus deformity requiring further correction and foot stiffness, though active toe movements are possible.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: De-tethering was performed in the 1st year of life.
Complete peritalar release for neurogenic right clubfoot performed at age 1.
Open reduction of the right hip at age 2, with femoral osteotomy and Pemberton acetabuloplasty.
21-08-2012: Resection of L3-4-5 hemivertebrae and posterior fusion L2–S1; spica cast applied.
Hardware (plates and K-wires) removed 8 months later (July 2013).
At last follow-up, the girl can walk independently using a shoelift of 7 cm.
The right hip joint is still well-reduced.
Recurrence of equinovarus deformity of the right foot, needing additional surgical correction.
Leg length discrepancy and hypotrophy of the right lower limb observed after surgery.

notes_on_uncertainty: The exact timing of definitive diagnosis is not documented; diagnosis of hemimyelomeningocele and spondylocostal dysplasia was established early, prior to multiple surgeries. Time-period categorizations are approximate relative to inferred early diagnosis. No explicit quality-of-life scales were reported; functional status and symptom burden were inferred from clinical descriptions.
